gemma4:26b-nvfp4.when: Fast-context chat model (MLX works for gemma). Use when you want to paste big text and get quick answers. NOT a coding-agent replacement.
gemma4:26b-nvfp4.speed: 558 tok/s prompt eval (5x GGUF), 41 tok/s decode

gemma4:e4b.when: Edge 4B gemma variant — untested for role. Multimodal capable (text+image).

llama3.1:8b.when: General chat fallback when you want a non-MLX / non-Gemma flavor. 14 months old; keep only if you have prompts tuned to llama3.

qwen2.5-coder:1.5b.when: Cron: rename-claude-sessions title generator. Tiny, 152 tok/s.

qwen2.5-coder:7b.when: Fast coder fallback when qwen3-coder feels heavy.

qwen3-coder:latest.when: Primary coding agent (aider/pi). Tool-calling strong; decode fast.

qwen3.5:9b.when: untagged — annotate when we know its role

gemma4:12b-nvfp4.when: Dense 12B, MLX. Pulled to test the Gemma 4 12B email pitch — did not beat gemma4:26b-nvfp4.
gemma4:12b-nvfp4.speed: 18 tok/s warm decode, 337 tok/s prompt eval
gemma4:12b-nvfp4.tested: Dense 12B slower per token than 26B MoE (4B active). Aider bug-fix 123s 4/4; pi bug-fix 176s 0/4 — pi diagnosed the bugs then never called the edit tool, leaked <|channel>thought artifacts.

gemma4:31b.tested: Thinking-model + dense architecture double-whammy. Burns ~150 tokens on internal reasoning every turn, then generates the answer at 6.5 tok/s. Unusable for interactive work. Reclaimed 19 GB. gemma4:26b-nvfp4 MoE covers the gemma role better in every way.

gemma4:latest.tested: Superseded by gemma4:26b-nvfp4 (MLX): 558 tok/s prompt eval (5x this) at similar decode speed. Same underlying 26B-a4b-it model; MLX quant is strictly faster on M2.

qwen2.5-coder:1.5b-base.when: Base model (not chat-tuned). Pulled by mistake for cron use.
qwen2.5-coder:1.5b-base.tested: Base model has no instruction/chat tuning — can't follow prompts or tool-call formats. qwen2.5-coder:1.5b (chat-tuned) is the right variant for cron title generation.

qwen2.5-coder:7b-base.tested: Base model — no chat tuning. qwen2.5-coder:7b is the usable version.

qwen3.5:0.8b-nvfp4.when: Small MLX candidate to replace qwen2.5-coder:1.5b for cron title generation.
qwen3.5:0.8b-nvfp4.tested: Confirms MLX-prompt-eval-bottleneck on M2 is architecture-family-wide, not just the 35B: 100 tok/s prompt eval vs qwen2.5-coder:1.5b GGUF's 807. Decode is faster (182 vs 156) but for cron-style small-prompt tasks the prompt-eval loss dominates. Reclaimed 1 GB.

qwen3.5:35b-a3b-coding-nvfp4.when: Ollama's MLX-flagship coding model. Advertised 2x speedup vs GGUF.
qwen3.5:35b-a3b-coding-nvfp4.tested: qwen3.5+NVFP4+MLX combo hits a broken slow path on M2 — prompt eval 3 tok/s vs qwen3-coder GGUF's 110. Thinking-model verbosity compounds it (362-832 tokens per response). Blog's 2x speedup is M5-only (GPU Neural Accelerators). Reclaimed 22 GB.
